Honestly disrespectful using a hypothetical Popovich to shame Mitch Johnson for making the finals during his first season as head coach. Pop lost the 2013 finals by benching Tim Duncan on 2 consecutive defensive possessions while league officials were roping off the court to hand him the trophy. Popovich got bounced in the first round as the one seed in 2011 by refusing to play Splitter. Pop is the greatest coach in league history imo, but he was far from perfect. Mitch Johnson has a long way to go to be anywhere close to Pop’s achievements, but he has just as far to go to match Pop’s failures too.

This playoff run was lightning in a bottle but the finals really exposed this teams’ weaknesses for everybody to see. The team has all the talent but keep the score close enough and they’ll beat themselves.

The greater threat was the 3 pointer from Brunson, with the amount of time left, you wouldn't expect there to be time for a rebound and second shot. The default move is to wind clock down and then take on last shot at it to deny them a chance to score. I was surprised at the depth and range of that shot. Brunson taking the shot so early in the time clock and from so deep was a *to me* unexpected move. It had possibly intentional effect of pulling Wemby out to give OG a chance at the rebound/tip-in. The luck factor was that a shot 31 feet away doesn't typically have such a soft bounce. The gifted athlete part was timing that jump and the legendary part was keeping such a soft touch when his adrenaline was at 1000x. Ask Josh Hart about it. Or Wemby. I want Josh Hart to have a chance to get the go ahead score next game. He doesn't need it, it won't change narrative of how awesome he is or how much we love him, just got sense in that interview that he feels he let himself down. And to that I say, Mr. Hart, give yourself some credit, your rebounds won game 1, you had plenty of other clutch moments in this game, and bro, it was closing seconds of a NBA finals historic comeback.wemby choked 2 free throws and threw ball away to lose game 2.
